title: BUG: Add delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table
body:
Per the PDF specification, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters. The current escaping mapping (NameObject.renumber_table) handles several special characters, but it does not include angle brackets, square brackets, or curly braces, which are also delimiters. As a result, names containing these characters could be serialized without proper escaping, potentially producing invalid PDFs when appending/merging content.

Proposed change:
- Extend NameObject.renumber_table to escape the characters: <, >, [, ], {, }
  - Mappings would be: '<' -> '#3C', '>' -> '#3E', '[' -> '#5B', ']' -> '#5D', '{' -> '#7B', '}' -> '#7D'
  - This aligns with the existing escaping approach for other characters (e.g., '#', '(', ')', '/', '%') and ensures any name containing these delimiters is safely serialized.

Impact:
- Serialization of Name objects will consistently escape these additional delimiters, preventing creation of invalid PDFs in edge cases where names contain such characters.
- No public API changes; behavior is more broadly correct and spec-compliant.

Notes:
- A minimal patch should only add the six key-value pairs to NameObject.renumber_table in pypdf/generic/_base.py.
- Consider adding/adjusting unit tests to cover renumbering of names containing these delimiters (e.g., "/Name<", "/Name[abc]", etc.).

step_plan.summary: Bug fix: Extend NameObject escaping to cover additional delimiter characters in PDF name tokens and add tests to verify escaping and parsing behavior.

steps.operation: Extend NameObject.renumber to escape six additional delimiter characters by updating the renumber_table dictionary
steps.reason: Add mappings: '<'->'#3C', '>'->'#3E', '['->'#5B', ']'->'#5D', '{'->'#7B', '}'->'#7D' so that all PDF name delimiters are properly escaped per spec; aligns with existing escaping approach for other characters
steps.side_effects: Low risk; internal escaping change; may require updates to existing tests that assert specific renumber outputs; no public API change

steps.operation: Add unit tests to cover renumber escaping for new delimiters and parsing back from escaped sequences
steps.reason: Verify escaping for each new delimiter and round-trip parsing; ensures correctness and spec compliance
steps.side_effects: Introduces new tests; ensures compatibility with parsing of escaped sequences; may require updates if internal parsing API changes
